title: 10 best herbal tinctures for post nasal drip
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for post nasal drip are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are taken orally to alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip. These tinctures typically include a combination of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant herbs that help reduce inflammation, fight infection, and soothe irritation in the throat and nasal passages. Examples of such herbs are goldenseal, Echinacea, licorice root, wild indigo, and fenugreek. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from post nasal drip, leading to improved sleep quality, reduced sinus pressure, and overall enhanced well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with post nasal drip because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and narrow the blood vessels, thus alleviating excessive mucus production. Its expectorant qualities also help to clear the respiratory tract by promoting the expulsion of phlegm and reducing congestion. Moreover, yarrow tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off any potential infections causing post nasal drip, providing relief and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties help to tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing post nasal drip. This is due to the presence of tannins in yarrow.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids in yarrow.

Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat post nasal drip caused by infections. This is due to the presence of lactones and essential oils in yarrow.

Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow tincture helps to relax and soothe muscle spasms in the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of post nasal drip. This is due to the presence of lactones in yarrow.
Diaphoretic properties: Yarrow tincture promotes sweating, helping to eliminate toxins and reduce congestion in the nasal passages. This is due to the presence of volatile oils in yarrow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or use 1 ounce of dried yarrow, and chop coarsely.
Combine herbs with 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, preferably amber-colored, for up to 5 years. Take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) 3 times daily for post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory flavonoids, yarrow flowers can help reduce post nasal drip and soothe irritated mucous membranes.
Leaves: High in volatile oils, yarrow leaves can provide relief from congestion and help alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip.
Stems: Containing tannins that have astringent properties, yarrow stems can help tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing post nasal drip.
Seeds: With high levels of essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a slick gel when it comes into contact with water. This gel-like substance can coat and soothe the throat, providing relief from irritation and inflammation. Additionally, marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate congestion. By addressing both the symptoms and the root cause of post nasal drip, marshmallow tincture can provide effective and natural relief for those suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tincture creates a protective film on mucous membranes, helping to soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms (active constituents
Expectorant: It helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to eliminate excess mucus caused by post nasal drip (active constituents
Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages and throat, providing relief from post nasal drip discomfort (active constituents
Antitussive: It helps suppress coughing, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits associated with post nasal drip (active constituents
Immunomodulatory: Marshmallow tincture supports a healthy immune response, helping the body fight off infections that can cause or worsen post nasal drip (active constituents
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried marshmallow root into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Securely seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
Take 30 drops of the tincture 3 times a day for post nasal drip, as directed by a healthcare professional.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Marshmallow flowers contain mucilage, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation associated with post nasal drip.
Leaves: Marshmallow leaves are rich in mucilage and flavonoids, making them effective in reducing inflammation and providing relief from symptoms of post nasal drip.
Roots: The roots of marshmallow plants contain high amounts of mucilage and starch, which can help soothe irritated mucous membranes and alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds are mucilaginous and have demulcent properties, which can help lubricate and soothe the respiratory tract, providing relief from post nasal drip.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden angelica tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can provide relief from excessive mucus production and irritation in the nasal passages. The herb's active compounds, such as coumarins and essential oils, work together to soothe inflammation in the respiratory tract, reducing the production of mucus that can lead to post nasal drip. Furthermore, angelica tincture can help stimulate the removal of excess mucus from the respiratory system, promoting clearer breathing and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Angelica tincture can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from post nasal drip. This property is due to the presence of essential oils such as alpha-pinene and limonene.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture can reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating irritation and discomfort caused by post nasal drip. This is attributed to the presence of coumarins and furanocoumarins.
Antimicrobial: Angelica tincture can help fight off infections that can exacerbate post nasal drip. This is due to the presence of essential oils like alpha-pinene and limonene, as well as coumarins.
Immunostimulant: The tincture can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the causes of post nasal drip. This is due to the presence of polysaccharides, which stimulate the immune system.

Nervine: Angelica tincture can help relax the nervous system, reducing stress and tension that can worsen symptoms of post nasal drip. This is due to the presence of essential oils like linalool and geraniol, which have calming effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the Angelica plant roots, cleaning them thoroughly and letting them dry for 24 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and place them in a clean jar, filling it up to half with the chopped roots and the other half with 80 proof vodka.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any remaining liquid.
Store the final Angelica tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dry place. Take 1-2 dropperfuls up to three times a day for post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce post-nasal drip symptoms.
Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants that can soothe irritated throat and nasal passages.
Leaves: Have expectorant properties, helping to clear excess mucus from the respiratory tract.
Flowers: Contain volatile oils that can help alleviate congestion and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, providing anti-inflammatory support for post-nasal drip.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains active compounds, such as echinacea, that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion and post nasal drip. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that can cause post nasal drip. By reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system, eastern purple coneflower tincture can provide relief from post nasal drip and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate post nasal drip by limiting irritation and swelling. This property is due to the presence of chrysoplenols and luteolin.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to post nasal drip. The active constituents responsible for this property include isobutyl angelate and chlorogenic acid.

Expectorant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture promotes the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear post nasal drip. The expectorant property is attributed to the presence of polyacetylenes and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: The tincture supports the immune system, enabling the body to better fight off infections that can cause post nasal drip. The immune-boosting property is primarily due to the presence of phenolic acids and alkamides.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture contains antioxidants that protect the respiratory tract from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of post nasal drip. The antioxidant property is attributed to the presence of caffeic acid and rutin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests or disease.
Roughly chop 2 ounces of dried herb or 4 ounces of fresh herb, then place in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour enough vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) into the jar to completely cover the plant material, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the mixture gently every few days.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) up to 3 times a day as needed for post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing relief for post nasal drip.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves can help alleviate congestion and soothe irritated throats.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, buds can help clear nasal passages and reduce post nasal drip symptoms.
Roots: Containing phenolic acids and other active compounds, roots can help reduce inflammation and support respiratory health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains active compounds that can help reduce inflammation and strengthen the immune system. These compounds include eleutherosides, saponins, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects. By reducing inflammation in the nasal passages, Siberian ginseng tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of post nasal drip, such as a constant need to clear the throat and irritation in the back of the throat. Additionally, a stronger immune system can help the body fight off infections that may be causing post nasal drip, providing further relief.
remedy_properties: Immune system stimulation: Siberian ginseng tincture can help strengthen the immune system, which is important for post nasal drip as it can help the body fight off infections and reduce inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, eleutherosides and polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Siberian ginseng has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip, due to the presence of ginsenosides.

Expectorant properties: Siberian ginseng tincture can help thin and expel mucus, making it easier to eliminate excess mucus from the respiratory tract, thus providing relief from post nasal drip, as a result of its saponin content.

Adaptogenic properties: Siberian ginseng is known as an adaptogen, which means it can help the body cope with stress and fatigue. This can be beneficial for post nasal drip, as stress and fatigue can exacerbate symptoms, and is attributed to eleutherosides and ginsenosides.

Antimicrobial activity: Siberian ginseng has antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against the harmful bacteria that can contribute to post nasal drip, because of its essential oils and phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Siberian ginseng root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or pure grain alcohol over the ginseng, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished Siberian ginseng tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle at room temperature, away from direct sunlight. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Siberian ginseng are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for post nasal drip due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Siberian ginseng contain active compounds that help alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip, such as congestion and irritation.
Seeds: The seeds of Siberian ginseng are used in tinctures for their adaptogenic properties, which can help the body cope with stress and strengthen the immune system.
Bark: The bark of Siberian ginseng contains beneficial compounds that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation associated with post nasal drip.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps with post nasal drip because of its potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Derived from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, this herbal tincture contains eucalyptus oil, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and irritation. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory system, providing relief from post nasal drip. Its soothing effects on the throat and nasal passages make Tasmanian blue gum tincture a popular natural remedy for those suffering from post nasal drip and other respiratory ailments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tincture reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, which helps alleviate post nasal drip by limiting irritation and swelling. This property is due to the presence of eucalyptol and tannins.

Expectorant: The tincture aids in thinning and expelling mucus, providing relief from post nasal drip by clearing excess mucus from the throat and nasal passages. The active constituents responsible for this property are cineole and alpha-pinene.

Antimicrobial: Tasmanian blue gum tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat post nasal drip caused by infections. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and globulol.

Decongestant: The tincture helps in reducing congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip by promoting better breathing and mucus drainage. The decongestant property is attributed to the presence of alpha-pinene and limonene.

Analgesic: Tasmanian blue gum tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help manage discomfort associated with post nasal drip. The active constituents responsible for this property include eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and globulol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from a Tasmanian blue gum tree, selecting young, bright green leaves for optimal potency.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of leaves into small pieces, then place them in a clean glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 16 ounces of 80-proof or higher alcohol, such as vodka, over the leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for a minimum of 2 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After the extraction period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid to extract all the beneficial compounds. The resulting tincture can be stored in a dark glass bottle and used as needed for post nasal drip relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain eucalyptus oil, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the nasal passages.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful for soothing irritated throats and reducing post nasal drip.
Bark: Rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing post nasal drip.
Stems: Contain essential oils and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, useful for alleviating symptoms of post nasal drip.

Additionally, licorice tincture can help relieve coughing and reduce irritation caused by post nasal drip, providing relief and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Licorice tincture helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus caused by post nasal drip. This property is important because it helps alleviate congestion and reduces the frequency of coughing. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from irritation and swelling caused by post nasal drip. This property is important because it helps soothe sore throats and reduce irritation caused by excessive mucus. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Demulcent: Licorice tincture forms a protective film over the mucous membranes, providing a soothing effect and reducing irritation caused by post nasal drip. This property is important because it helps alleviate discomfort and pain in the throat and respiratory tract. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and polysaccharides.

remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.

After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use 1-2 dropperfuls up to 4 times a day to relieve post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce post nasal drip.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help soothe irritated throat and nasal passages.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain coumarins, which have antispasmodic properties that can help relieve coughing and congestion.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains triterpenoid saponins, which have expectorant properties that can help clear phlegm and mucus from the respiratory tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the tincture can help soothe irritation in the throat and reduce the frequency of coughing, providing relief from the discomfort of post nasal drip. The herb's natural properties can provide a more gentle and holistic approach to managing post nasal drip, making it a popular natural remedy for many people.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tincture reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating irritation caused by post nasal drip. This property is important because it helps soothe the throat and reduce discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and flavonoids.

Expectorant: English ivy tincture aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear the airways and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms. This property is important because it reduces mucus buildup and promotes easier breathing. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and terpenes.

Antimicrobial: English ivy tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, reducing the risk of respiratory infections that can exacerbate post nasal drip. This property is important because it helps prevent the worsening of symptoms and promotes overall respiratory health. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenes.

Antispasmodic: English ivy tincture relaxes smooth muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing and spasms associated with post nasal drip. This property is important because it alleviates discomfort and promotes relaxation. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, flavonoids, and terpenes.

Mucolytic: English ivy tincture thins and breaks down mucus, making it easier to expel from the respiratory tract and alleviating post nasal drip symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest English ivy leaves from a mature, healthy plant, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Chop or grind the leaves to increase their surface area, then place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol into the jar, ensuring the leaves are completely submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, squeezing out the excess, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of English ivy are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for post nasal drip due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and mucus production in the respiratory tract.
Stems: The stems of English ivy contain saponins, which have been shown to have expectorant and anti-spasmodic properties, making them useful in tinctures for post nasal drip.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of English ivy contain high levels of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, making them a popular choice for medicinal tinctures for post nasal drip.

Flowers: The flowers of English ivy contain essential oils and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for post nasal drip.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, which can alleviate the symptoms of post nasal drip. Additionally, goldenseal has been shown to have immune-boosting effects, which can help the body fight off infections that may be causing or exacerbating post nasal drip. The tincture form of goldenseal allows for quick absorption into the bloodstream, providing fast-acting relief. Furthermore, goldenseal can help soothe a sore throat and cough, which are often associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goldenseal tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its alkaloid content like berberine, helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip.
Astringent: The astringent property of goldenseal tincture, from tannins, tightens and strengthens mucous membranes, reducing post nasal drip and soothing irritation.
Antimicrobial: Goldenseal tincture's antimicrobial property, also from berberine, helps fight bacterial and viral infections causing post nasal drip, promoting a quicker recovery.
Immune-boosting: Goldenseal tincture's ability to boost the immune system, due to its isoquinoline alkaloids like hydrastine, helps the body combat the underlying causes of post nasal drip.
Mucus thinning: Goldenseal tincture's mucus-thinning property, from saponins, helps break down and clear excess mucus, providing relief from post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried goldenseal root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the goldenseal, ensuring all pieces are covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle and take 30-60 drops up to 3 times daily for post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Goldenseal roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for post nasal drip due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of goldenseal are rich in alkaloids, making them effective in soothing mucous membranes and reducing excess mucus production.
Leaves: Goldenseal leaves have been used in tinctures to treat post nasal drip, as they contain berberine, which helps to reduce inflammation and fight infections.

Flowers: Although not as commonly used as the roots or rhizomes, goldenseal flowers can be included in tinctures for post nasal drip, as they contain compounds that help to soothe irritated throat tissue.

Seeds: Goldenseal seeds are a less frequently used part of the plant, but they can be included in tinctures for post nasal drip due to their astringent properties, which help to tighten and tone mucous membranes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with post nasal drip because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Its expectorant properties can help loosen and expel mucus, providing relief from a constant tickle in the throat and coughing fits. Additionally, lavender has natural calming and relaxing effects, which can help alleviate stress and promote better sleep, further supporting the body's natural healing process. By providing these comprehensive benefits, English lavender tincture can offer a natural and effective solution for managing post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: English lavender tincture's antimicrobial properties help reduce the risk of post nasal drip caused by bacterial or viral infections, due to the presence of compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and eucalyptol.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory effects of lavender tincture help alleviate irritation and swelling in the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip, thanks to the active constituents like cineole and camphor.

Expectorant property: English lavender tincture's expectorant properties assist in thinning and expelling mucus, making it easier to clear the airways and reduce post nasal drip, due to the presence of compounds like eucalyptol and alpha-pinene.

Analgesic property: The analgesic properties of lavender tincture help soothe discomfort and pain associated with post nasal drip, as active constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate have natural pain-relieving qualities.

Immunomodulatory property: English lavender tincture supports a healthy immune response, which is crucial for preventing and managing post nasal drip caused by infections or allergies, due to the presence of compounds like 1,8-cineole and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh English lavender flowers, filling a clean, dry mason jar to 3/4 full.
Add 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged, covering by at least 1 inch.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dry jar or bottle, squeezing out all liquid.
Label the tincture with the ingredients and date, storing in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to 3 times a day as needed for post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in aromatic compounds, English lavender flowers are used in tinctures to help alleviate post nasal drip due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of English lavender contain therapeutic compounds, making them suitable for tinctures that help reduce inflammation and mucus production in post nasal drip.
Buds: High in essential oils, English lavender buds are used in tinctures to provide relief from post nasal drip symptoms, thanks to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat post nasal drip with herbal tinctures is by using a mix of anti-inflammatory and expectorant herbs. Goldenseal, for instance, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while licorice root acts as an expectorant, thinning and expelling mucus. Echinacea can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off any underlying infections causing the post nasal drip. Mix a few drops of these tinctures in a glass of water and drink it two to three times a day for best results. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to post nasal drip that are treated with herbal tinctures include sinus congestion, sore throats, and coughs. Tinctures made from herbs such as goldenseal, echinacea, and licorice root can help to reduce inflammation, fight infection, and soothe irritated tissues. For example, goldenseal has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate sinus congestion and post-nasal drip, while echinacea can boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Licorice root, on the other hand, can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal tinctures to ensure safety and effectiveness.
